ingredients.item: 1 cup (240 ml) plus 1 tablespoon vegetable oil, divided
ingredients.original: 1 cup (240 ml) plus 1 tablespoon vegetable oil, divided

ingredients.item: 2 cups (240 g) plus 1 tablespoon all-purpose flour, divided
ingredients.original: 2 cups (240 g) plus 1 tablespoon all-purpose flour, divided

ingredients.item: 1 Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C) and position the rack in the middle. Grease a 10-cup (2.4 L) Bundt pan with 1 tablespoon of the oil, then dust it with 1 tablespoon of the flour. Tap the outside of the pan to circulate the flour so that it covers the inside surface.
ingredients.original: 1 Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C) and position the rack in the middle. Grease a 10-cup (2.4 L) Bundt pan with 1 tablespoon of the oil, then dust it with 1 tablespoon of the flour. Tap the outside of the pan to circulate the flour so that it covers the inside surface.

ingredients.item: 2 Zest the oranges (avoiding the white pith) into a small bowl to yield roughly 4 to 5 tablespoons of orange zest. Juice the oranges into a separate small bowl to yield ¾ cup (180 ml) of juice.
ingredients.original: 2 Zest the oranges (avoiding the white pith) into a small bowl to yield roughly 4 to 5 tablespoons of orange zest. Juice the oranges into a separate small bowl to yield ¾ cup (180 ml) of juice.

ingredients.item: 3 In a large bowl, whisk together the remaining 2 cups (240 g) flour, the baking powder, salt, granulated sugar, and cardamom (if using). Whisk in the orange zest.
ingredients.original: 3 In a large bowl, whisk together the remaining 2 cups (240 g) flour, the baking powder, salt, granulated sugar, and cardamom (if using). Whisk in the orange zest.

ingredients.item: 4 In a medium bowl, whisk the remaining 1 cup (240 ml) oil, the eggs, and orange juice until combined.
ingredients.original: 4 In a medium bowl, whisk the remaining 1 cup (240 ml) oil, the eggs, and orange juice until combined.

ingredients.item: 5 Gradually fold the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients using a silicone spatula until the wet and dry ingredients are just combined and you no longer see flour streaks. Do not overmix the batter. Pour the batter into the prepared Bundt pan.
ingredients.original: 5 Gradually fold the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients using a silicone spatula until the wet and dry ingredients are just combined and you no longer see flour streaks. Do not overmix the batter. Pour the batter into the prepared Bundt pan.

ingredients.item: 6 Bake for 45 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. Let cool for 10 minutes, then carefully flip onto a cake plate or stand. Let cool completely.
ingredients.original: 6 Bake for 45 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. Let cool for 10 minutes, then carefully flip onto a cake plate or stand. Let cool completely.

ingredients.item: 7 Dust with the powdered sugar, slice, and serve.
ingredients.original: 7 Dust with the powdered sugar, slice, and serve.
